name: Ascent to La Serrota — circular from Cepeda de la Mora
name_original: Subida a La Serrota — circular desde Cepeda de la Mora
region_intro: Hiking around Cepeda La Mora offers diverse landscapes in the province of Ávila, Spain. The region is situated at an altitude of 1,504 meters in the upper Alberche river valley, on the southern slope of the La Serrota mountain range. This geographical position provides varied terrain, including higher altitude trails, river ecosystems, and natural features like waterfalls. The landscape transitions from lower scrubland to areas with meadows and potato fields, leading to colder, snowier conditions at higher elevations. Best hiking trails around Cepeda La Mora The most popular hiking route is Ascent to La Serrota — circular from Cepeda de la Mora, a difficult 12.5 miles (20.0 km) trail that takes 6 hours 32 minutes to complete, offering expansive views of the Sierra de Gredos. Another top favourite among local hikers is Las Chorreras de San Martín del Pimpollar Trail, a moderate 6.8 miles (10.9 km) path. This route features waterfalls, bridges, and views of the Sierra de Gredos. Local hikers also love the San Martín del Pimpollar — circular by Cerro Redondo, a 7.0 miles (11.2 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 3 hours 9 minutes. Hiking around Cepeda La Mora is defined by the La Serrota mountain range, the Alberche river valley, and natural waterfalls. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Cepeda La Mora are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.25 stars from more than 5 reviews. More than 80 hikers have used komoot to explore Cepeda La Mora's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Cepeda La Mora?
A: Cepeda La Mora offers a diverse range of landscapes. You'll find yourself in the upper Alberche river valley, with pastures and meadows, and on the southern slopes of the La Serrota mountain range. As you ascend, the terrain transitions, offering panoramic views, especially of the Sierra de Gredos. The area also features natural attractions like waterfalls.

Q: Are there hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Cepeda La Mora?
A: Yes, Cepeda La Mora offers trails for various fitness levels. There are easy trails, typically up to 2 hours long with up to 300 meters of elevation gain, which are suitable for all fitness levels and families. These paths are generally easily accessible.

Q: Can I find trails with waterfalls in Cepeda La Mora?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its natural waterfalls. A notable route featuring these is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1343103081/las-chorreras-de-san-martin-del-pimpollar-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Chorreras de San Martín del Pimpollar Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which includes two drops of four and five meters, along with bridges and stunning views of the Sierra de Gredos.

Q: What are the main natural attractions or viewpoints along the hiking routes?
A: Hikers can enjoy several natural attractions and viewpoints. The area offers expansive views of the Sierra de Gredos from higher elevations, such as those found on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1143078131/ascent-to-la-serrota-circular-from-cepeda-de-la-mora" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to La Serrota&lt;/a&gt;. Other notable points include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1711130" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chía Pass (1,663 m)&lt;/a&gt; and various mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5437098" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de la Cañada del Horno&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5758478" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado de la Honda&lt;/a&gt;, which offer scenic vistas.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available in Cepeda La Mora?
A: Yes, several circular routes are available. For instance, the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1143078131/ascent-to-la-serrota-circular-from-cepeda-de-la-mora" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to La Serrota&lt;/a&gt; is a circular trail. Another popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1343130660/san-martin-del-pimpollar-circular-by-cerro-redondo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Martín del Pimpollar — circular by Cerro Redondo&lt;/a&gt;, offering varied terrain.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Cepeda La Mora?
A: The climate in Cepeda La Mora varies with elevation. Lower areas experience mild, dry summers, while higher altitudes, especially towards La Serrota, have colder winters with abundant snowfall. For most hikers, spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Winter hiking is possible, particularly at higher elevations, but requires appropriate gear due to snow.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Cepeda La Mora?
A: The trails in Cepeda La Mora are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.25 stars. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, from river valleys to mountain slopes, and the stunning views of the Sierra de Gredos. The presence of natural features like waterfalls also receives positive feedback.

Q: How many hiking trails are there in Cepeda La Mora?
A: There are 9 hiking trails available in Cepeda La Mora, catering to various difficulty levels from easy to difficult.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Yes, for experienced hikers, there are difficult routes over 5 hours long or with more than 900 meters of elevation gain. These demand very good physical condition, sturdy shoes, and alpine experience. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1343148798/el-puerto-del-pico-from-san-martin-del-pimpollar-circular-through-the-sierra-de-gredos-gr-10" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Puerto del Pico circular route from San Martín del Pimpollar&lt;/a&gt;, exploring the Sierra de Gredos.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on the trails?
A: The region is home to various wild game, including hares, rabbits, and partridges. The rivers, such as the Alberche, also contain trout, contributing to the area's diverse ecosystem.

Q: Are there any specific trails that offer views of the Sierra de Gredos?
A: Many trails in Cepeda La Mora offer views of the Sierra de Gredos, particularly those at higher elevations. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1143078131/ascent-to-la-serrota-circular-from-cepeda-de-la-mora" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to La Serrota&lt;/a&gt; provides expansive views, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1343103081/las-chorreras-de-san-martin-del-pimpollar-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Chorreras de San Martín del Pimpollar Trail&lt;/a&gt; also features stunning vistas of the range.
tips: The Collado de la Honda is the final part of an old glacier that ends at this point. The glacier is the result of ancient times when the glacial movements were more southern and this is an example of how in this area of the middle of the Meseta there were.
The Serrota is a mountain that reaches 2,292 meters in height, which means that you need to watch the time of its ascent, where the snow covers the area completely with low temperatures.
